intro: Odontotrichum sinuatum is a plant species in the family Campanulaceae, commonly known as the Himalayan tooth-leaf or Odontotrichum. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its potential in treating various health issues such as fever, inflammation, and digestive problems, due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Odontotrichum sinuatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Odontotrichum sinuatum is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the formulation of ointments and creams for topical application.
